summary: Soundboard recording (good overall) with a minor glitch in Let It Grow, highlighted by an outstanding Dark Star that ranks among the tour’s best.
review: The set is praised for its powerful, flowing Dark Star – several reviewers call it one of the top performances of the ’70s. The soundboard source delivers clear audio, though one listener notes a strange cut in Let It Grow that adds a spooky moment to the tape. Overall the show is considered solid and worth listening to.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong consensus on Dark Star’s excellence (70% weight) plus generally good soundboard quality with only a minor recording glitch (30% weight) yields an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and balanced overall; a noticeable splice/cut in Let It Grow creates a brief, spooky artifact but does not detract significantly from the listening experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is anchored by a stellar Dark Star; aside from a minor recording hiccup, the performance flows smoothly and maintains strong energy throughout.
